assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Atomic Recreation: Each TPU slice (256 chips) gets recreated entirely in a single step. There's no rolling upgrade — all 256 chips restart simultaneously per slice.'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Since you have 4 interconnected slices, a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL 4 slices. This is due to the tight coupling in multislice environments.'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explains that training job will experience 'complete restart' and that the job 'spans all slices and can't survive partial failure', clearly indicating interruption.

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'During recreation, if insufficient TPU v5e resources exist in your zone, the slice will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 'If capacity is constrained, it could be hours.'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Save checkpoint manually during a natural stopping point' as part of the controlled restart strategy and emphasizes checkpoint verification.

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Restart training from checkpoint' section and mentions 'Launch training job pointing to latest checkpoint' with validation steps for smooth resume.

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions and mentions 'Use multiple 30-day exclusions to defer maintenance until your training campaign completes.'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance exclusions to defer events and controlled restart timing, it does not specifically discuss using maintenance windows to schedule when the event occurs during planned breaks.
